ai_research.headline: Alibaba Qwen 3.6-Plus targets agentic coding — Microsoft ships first in-house MAI model family

items.title: Alibaba Qwen 3.6-Plus — 1M Context, Built for Agents
items.body: Alibaba released Qwen 3.6-Plus: text + agentic model with 1M token context, tuned for autonomous coding (frontend, repo-level engineering, terminal/GUI agents). Open access, ~$0.28/M tokens. Competing directly with Claude and GPT on code agent benchmarks at a fraction of the cost.

items.title: Microsoft MAI Models — First In-House Foundation Models
items.body: Microsoft shipped three in-house models via Azure AI Foundry: MAI-Transcribe-1 (fast multilingual speech-to-text), MAI-Voice-1 (custom voice generation), and MAI-Image-2 (image and video generation). Microsoft is reducing dependence on OpenAI with its own model stack.

items.title: Zhipu GLM-5.1 — 744B MoE, Beats Opus on SWE-Bench
items.body: Zhipu AI dropped GLM-5.1: 744B parameter MoE (40B active), 200K context. Claims to beat Claude Opus 4.6 and GPT-5.4 on SWE-Bench Pro. Fully open (MIT), free self-host. This is the most capable open-weight coding model released to date.

items.title: Open-Weight April — Google Gemma 4 + PrismML Bonsai
items.body: Google Gemma 4 family (27B, 26B MoE, edge variants) launched April 1 under Apache 2.0. PrismML Bonsai 8B is a 1-bit quantized model (14x smaller) strong on chat. The open-weight ecosystem is exploding — April has been the biggest month for open releases in 2026.

ai_research.learn_today: Read the Qwen 3.6-Plus release details — Alibaba is aggressively targeting the agentic coding space at ultra-low prices. If you're building agents, this model family deserves evaluation.

openclaw_agents.headline: MCP + LangGraph dominate the agent stack — Claude 'Managed Agents' hits public beta

items.title: Claude 'Managed Agents' — Public Beta
items.body: Anthropic launched Managed Agents in public beta: a production-ready harness for deploying scalable agents. Slashes prototype-to-prod time to days. Includes built-in guardrails, tool access, and conversation management. Now available for Claude API users.
items.try_this: Check Anthropic's docs if you're deploying agents on Claude — Managed Agents replaces custom scaffolding.

items.title: MCP + LangGraph = The 2026 Agent Stack
items.body: DZone published a detailed guide on MCP + LangGraph as the dominant production agent pattern. LangGraph #1 in 2026 framework rankings (27K monthly searches). MCP protocol now supported in 12+ frameworks. LangChain reports 57% org adoption of agent frameworks (up from 51%). The paradigm has shifted from RAG to agentic workflows.
items.try_this: If you're building agents, start with LangGraph + MCP. Pick one manual task and wrap it as an MCP server this week.

items.title: Agentic Frameworks Up 340% YoY on X
items.body: X discussions show agentic frameworks (LangChain, CrewAI, AutoGen) up 340% year-over-year. A 25K-task study found self-organizing agents (no roles/hierarchy) beat structured frameworks by 14%. The agent economy is accelerating: VISA launched 'Intelligent Commerce Connect' for agent payments, Microsoft shipped APM (Agent Package Manager).
items.try_this: Watch for the VISA agent payments integration — autonomous shopping agents are becoming real.

items.title: Security Alert: Autonomous Agents = New Attack Surface
items.body: CrowdStrike warns that autonomous agents make endpoints the new security control plane. As agents gain tool access and autonomy, security models must evolve. Agent-to-agent trust, sandboxing, and audit trails are becoming critical.
items.try_this: Audit your agent's tool permissions — least privilege applies to AI agents just like human users.

openclaw_agents.try_today: Evaluate LangGraph + MCP for your next agent build. If you're still using raw API calls without structured tool discovery, you're leaving reliability and scalability on the table.

tesla.note: Stock data unavailable (yfinance API error). TSLA reportedly consolidating around $337 with limited downside to ~$320 and upside potential to $370-380. Key catalysts: Q1 earnings April 22, Cybercab production starting this month, FSD v14.3 reviews ongoing. China Q1 exports hit 100,600 (highest ever), Giga Shanghai March exports +529% YoY. Reuters reports a new smaller, cheaper electric SUV in early development. JPMorgan bearish note (potential 60% drop) drew pushback from Elon.

tesla_spacex_xai.spacex: SpaceX IPO speculation continues — potential $1.75-2T valuation with possible investor briefings this month (analyst day rumored April 21). No confirmed timeline. Tesla-SpaceX merger speculation persists but experts call concerns overblown. Bitget exchange teased a 'rocket' event for April 10. Terafab chip project with Tesla, xAI, and Intel progressing.
tesla_spacex_xai.xai: Grok continues integration into X platform: auto-translation for all posts worldwide and AI photo editor in iOS app. No new model release — xAI focusing on platform integrations and real-world deployment over new model announcements.

items.title: The Super Mario Galaxy Movie
items.note: Opened April 1 — 89% audience score, still drawing crowds. The Mario franchise keeps delivering.

items.title: Mermaid
items.note: Opened April 8 — 94% critics. Strong early reviews. Fresh pick for this week.

items.title: Exit 8
items.note: Opens April 10 — 94% critics score already. Based on the viral indie game. Weekend material.

items.title: The Blue Trail
items.note: Opened April 3 — 100% critics, 93% audience. The best-reviewed film in theaters right now.

items.title: The Drama
items.note: Opened April 3 — 77% critics, 80% audience. Solid ensemble piece with good word of mouth.

items.title: OpenAI Pivots Enterprise with 'Super App'
items.body: OpenAI raised at $852B valuation and launched a unified 'super app' combining chat, coding, search, and agents. Retreating from consumer experiments to focus on enterprise automation.

items.title: HubSpot Breeze: Outcome-Based Agent Pricing (April 14)
items.body: HubSpot switching Breeze AI agents to outcome-based pricing: $0.50/resolved convo, $1/qualified lead. No more flat subscription. Pay for results, not seats — this is where AI marketing pricing is headed.

items.title: Forbes: Off-the-Shelf AI Tools Are Commodity Now
items.body: Off-the-shelf AI marketing tools no longer provide competitive advantage. Everyone has the same ChatGPT-powered copy tool. Edge comes from custom integrations and proprietary data. Build internal AI layers.

items.title: Zapier's Updated AI Social Media Tools (April)
items.body: Top picks: Buffer ($5/mo), Publer ($4/mo with brand voice AI), FeedHive (content recycling), Predis.ai (full post generation). Enterprise: Hootsuite, Sprout Social ($199/mo+). Integration-first for agentic workflows.

macro.mood: Stock data unavailable (yfinance API error). BTC holding $71K. Markets cautious ahead of Tesla Q1 earnings (April 22) and potential SpaceX IPO filings.
